data.title: Ripple’s CEO take after the White House crypto summit: A level playing field
data.description:
Ripple CEO speaks after the White House hosted its first-ever crypto summit, signaling a shift in U.S. digital asset policy under President Trump.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ripples-ceo-take-after-the-wh-crypto-summit/

data.title: Solana Proposal Aims To Reduce Inflation Rate By 80%
data.description: According to BlockBeats, voting has commenced on Solana's SIMD-0228 proposal, which seeks to reduce the inflation rate of SOL by 80%. This development was reported by SolanaFloor on March 8.

data.title: Solana's SIMD-0228 proposal has opened for voting, aiming to reduce SOL inflation by 80%
data.description: According to SolanaFloor’s disclosure on the X platform, Solana’s SIMD-0228 proposal has begun voting, which can reduce the SOL inflation rate by 80%.

data.title: Solana Validators Vote On Proposal Affecting Network Inflation
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Solana validators have commenced voting on the SIMD-0228 proposal, which could impact network inflation. Data from Dune indicates that 7.3726% of votes are in favor, 1.1791% are against, and 91.4481% have yet to vote. The voting period will conclude at epoch 755.

Previously reported by Foresight News, the SIMD-0228 proposal was authored by Tushar Jain and Vishal Kankani of Multicoin Capital, with support from Anza's Chief Economist Max Resnick. The proposal suggests adjusting the SOL issuance rate to a market-based solution. It includes measures such as reducing issuance if the SOL staking rate exceeds 50%, thereby lowering yields to deter staking. Conversely, if the staking rate falls below 50%, issuance would increase to boost yields and encourage staking. The minimum inflation rate is set at 0%, while the maximum will follow the current Solana issuance curve.

data.title: Crypto’s Kraken plans to go public in Q1 2026
data.description:
Kraken is finally making its move. The crypto exchange, officially known as Payward Inc., is planning to go public in the first quarter of 2026, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday following the White House’s crypto summit. After years of delays, mostly caused by regulatory crackdowns, the company is taking advantage of the friendlier […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/cryptos-kraken-plans-to-go-public-in-q1-2026/

data.title: Avalanche Foundation Board Members Resign Amid Uncertainty
data.description: According to PANews, former Avalanche Foundation director Omer announced on social media that he, along with board members Aytunç Yildizli and Vikram Nagrani, officially resigned from the foundation and its subsidiaries earlier this week. Aytunç ceased his executive functions on February 28, 2025, leaving the board in a state of uncertainty as they sought a path forward. Omer expressed that the decision to resign was not made lightly and was a painful necessity. He emphasized their passion, dedication, and commitment to Avalanche and its employees, acknowledging the hard work they have contributed to the ecosystem and community since joining the foundation. The collective resignation marks a challenging decision for the board members.

data.title: A whale released 36,156 SOLs that had been pledged for a year, earning $1.51 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale has released 36,156 SOL (US$5.05 million) after staking for a year, making a profit of US$1.51 million. 
Previously, the address pledged 32,999 SOL on February 10, 2024, and made a profit of 3,157 SOL from staking.

data.title: Avalanche Foundation's three directors resign
data.description: Omer, a former director of the Avalanche Foundation, posted on the X platform that he confirmed that three directors, including himself, Aytunç Yildizli and Vikram Nagrani, had officially resigned from the board of directors of the foundation and its subsidiaries at the beginning of this week. Among them, Executive Director Aytunç Yildizli stopped performing his duties on February 28, 2025, and the board has been on the sidelines since then, trying to find a way forward. Omer said, "During the past 18 months of my tenure, Aytunç Yildizli, Vikram Nagrani and I have promoted a number of important tasks, including establishing the brand image of the Avalanche Foundation, publishing the first annual report, launching incentive programs such as infraBUIDL () and infraBUIDL (AI), and developing the AiFRED project. We are also committed to strengthening internal coordination within the ecosystem and shifting the focus of the growth strategy to new market development and strategic cooperation. This collective voluntary resignation decision is "well-considered", and we will continue to pay attention to the development of the Avalanche ecosystem in the future. I wish the Avalanche ecosystem and community all the best."
